---
title: ""
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Pollen viability - summary

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "../")
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
pollen_viability <- read.csv("pollen_counting_results/pollen_counts.csv")
```

```{r}
# Define the pattern
# Matches ([Ss]?\d+x) or (\d+x) potentially without leading S
pattern <- "([Ss]?\\d+x)_([A-Z0-9])"

# Find matches
matches <- regexec(pattern, pollen_viability$filename)
match_list <- regmatches(pollen_viability$filename, matches)

# Handle cases where match failed (e.g. empty match)
ploidy_level <- sapply(match_list, function(x) if(length(x) >= 2) x[2] else "Unknown")
replication <- sapply(match_list, function(x) if(length(x) >= 3) x[3] else "Unknown")

# Calculate viability percent (column was renamed from total to total_counted)
# We handle both cases just in case
total_col <- if("total_counted" %in% names(pollen_viability)) "total_counted" else "total"
pollen_viability$viability_percent <- (pollen_viability$viable / pollen_viability[[total_col]]) * 100

# Create the final dataframe
pollen_viability2 <- data.frame(
    filename = pollen_viability$filename,
    viability_percent = pollen_viability$viability_percent,
    ploidy_level = ploidy_level,
    replication = replication
)

# cheking ploidy level
table(pollen_viability2$ploidy_level)

# repairing ploidy level naming typo to ensure consistency (2x, S2x, 4x, S4x)
pollen_viability2$ploidy_level[pollen_viability2$ploidy_level == "s2x"] <- "S2x"
pollen_viability2$ploidy_level[pollen_viability2$ploidy_level == "s4x"] <- "S4x"
# Note: 2x and 4x are kept as distinct levels
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
plot(as.factor(pollen_viability2$ploidy_level), as.numeric(pollen_viability2$viability_percent), ylim = c(0, 100), xlim = c(0, 5))

# zkusit biomodial?

p <- ggplot(pollen_viability2, aes(ploidy_level, viability_percent))
p + geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) + geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    theme_classic()
p + geom_violin() + geom_jitter(height = 0, width = 0.1, alpha = 0.5) +
    theme_classic() + labs(x = "Ploidy level", y = "Pollen viability (%)") + ggtitle("Pollen viability", subtitle = "Before excluding weird images")
```

# Summary statistics by ploidy

```{r}
# Display summary table
ploidy_summary <- aggregate(viability_percent ~ ploidy_level, data = pollen_viability2, 
                           FUN = function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sd = sd(x), n = length(x)))
ploidy_summary <- do.call(data.frame, ploidy_summary)
knitr::kable(ploidy_summary, caption = "Viability Summary by Ploidy Level", digits = 2)
```

# Particle size analysis

```{r}
# Load detailed measurements
if (file.exists("pollen_counting_results/particle_measurements.csv")) {
    particle_measurements <- read.csv("pollen_counting_results/particle_measurements.csv")

    # Filter for included particles only (fully visible/non-overlapping)
    measurable_particles <- particle_measurements[particle_measurements$is_excluded == "False", ]

    # Extract ploidy info
    measurable_matches <- regmatches(measurable_particles$filename, regexec(pattern, measurable_particles$filename))
    measurable_match_matrix <- do.call(rbind, measurable_matches)

    measurable_particles$ploidy_level <- measurable_match_matrix[, 2]
    # Standardize ploidy labels (S2x, S4x) while keeping 2x, 4x distinct
    measurable_particles$ploidy_level[measurable_particles$ploidy_level == "s2x"] <- "S2x"
    measurable_particles$ploidy_level[measurable_particles$ploidy_level == "s4x"] <- "S4x"

    # Create a sampled version of the data for the jitter points to speed up rendering
    # Boxplots will still use the full data for statistics
    max_pts <- 5000
    if (nrow(measurable_particles) > max_pts) {
        set.seed(42)
        measurable_sampled <- measurable_particles[sample(nrow(measurable_particles), max_pts), ]
        subtitle_sampled <- paste0("Excluding edge-touching/overlapping. Jitter points sampled to ", max_pts)
    } else {
        measurable_sampled <- measurable_particles
        subtitle_sampled <- "Excluding edge-touching/overlapping particles"
    }

    # Area comparison
    p_area <- ggplot(measurable_particles, aes(ploidy_level, area_px, fill = class)) +
        geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
        geom_jitter(data = measurable_sampled, aes(color = class), 
                    alpha = 0.1, position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.8)) +
        theme_classic() +
        labs(x = "Ploidy level", y = "Area (px²)", fill = "Viability", color = "Viability") +
        ggtitle("Particle Area Distribution by Ploidy Level and Viability", 
                subtitle = subtitle_sampled)
    print(p_area)

    # Diameter comparison
    p_diam <- ggplot(measurable_particles, aes(ploidy_level, diameter_px, fill = class)) +
        geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
        geom_jitter(data = measurable_sampled, aes(color = class), 
                    alpha = 0.1, position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.2, dodge.width = 0.8)) +
        theme_classic() +
        labs(x = "Ploidy level", y = "Equivalent Diameter (px)", fill = "Viability", color = "Viability") +
        ggtitle("Particle Diameter Distribution by Ploidy Level and Viability", 
                subtitle = subtitle_sampled)
    print(p_diam)
    
} else {
    print("Particle measurements file not found. Run sync_results.py first.")
}
```

## Low pollen viability samples

```{r}
# Filter for samples with less than 10% viability
low_viability <- pollen_viability2[pollen_viability2$viability_percent < 10, ]
low_viability <- low_viability[order(low_viability$viability_percent), ]

# Display as a table
knitr::kable(low_viability[, c("filename", "viability_percent", "ploidy_level")],
    caption = "Samples with <10% Viability",
    row.names = FALSE
)
```
